channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Sri Lanka increases fuel prices by 25% due to Middle East conflict and Strait of Hormuz closure
item.description: Sri Lanka has implemented a significant 25% rise in fuel prices effective Sunday, marking the second increase within two weeks amid escalating global energy pressures from the ongoing US-Israel war ag...

item.title: Maharashtra Opposition Calls for Inquiry into Ruling Alliance Ministers over Links with Arrested Godman Ashok Kharat
item.description: In a major political development in Maharashtra, the opposition parties have strongly demanded a thorough probe against several ministers and leaders from the ruling Mahayuti alliance who have alleged...

item.title: Woman arrested over murder of film sound manager Athuman Nyanza in Dar es Salaam
item.description: A tragic incident has shocked residents of Chanika area in Dar es Salaam after Athuman Nyanza, a well-known film sound manager, was allegedly killed by his partner and buried in a shallow grave near h...

item.title: La Trinidad Celebrates Community Spirit and Culture at the 2026 Strawberry Festival
item.description: La Trinidad, Benguet, celebrated its 2026 Strawberry Festival with a vibrant parade that showcased the town’s community spirit, culture, and traditions. The parade, which started early morning on Marc...

item.title: Woman arrested in connection with death of man found in Suffolk park
item.description: A woman has been arrested on suspicion of murder after a man was found unresponsive in Sparrow's Nest Gardens in Lowestoft, Suffolk, on Friday evening. Police responded to the scene at 10:13 pm and di...

item.title: Department of Agriculture secures P50 million for P5,000 fuel subsidy distribution to 9,570 mechanized farmers
item.description: The Department of Agriculture (DA) has announced that it will begin distributing a P5,000 fuel subsidy to eligible farmers to help offset the rising cost of fuel, particularly diesel, which has increa...

item.title: Highway near Bellingham closed indefinitely after landslides
item.description: A major highway near Bellingham, Washington, remains closed due to significant landslides, with no estimated reopening time provided. The landslides occurred in the area of Interstate 5, impacting tra...

item.title: Bev Steyn Takes Legal Action Against Nonku Williams Over Unpaid Loan
item.description: Beverley Steyn, star of 'The Real Housewives of Cape Town', has taken legal action against her former friend and 'The Real Housewives of Durban' star, Nonku Williams, demanding repayment of R125,000 s...

item.title: AI Dashcams to Detect Highway Defects Across 40,000 Km of National Highways
item.description: The National Highways Authority of India (NHAI) has decided to deploy AI-powered dashcam analytics services across nearly 40,000 km of highways under operation and maintenance contracts. This decision...

item.title: Oburu's call for economic justice and what it means for Kenya's power structures
item.description: The article examines Senate Minority Leader Oburu Oginga's recent call for economic justice and how it ties into broader questions of power, class and marginalisation in Kenya. It argues that Oburu is...

item.title: Asake Talks About the Freedom Money Gives Him and How It Solves Most of His Problems
item.description: In a recent interview with indie filmmaker and podcaster Korty EO, Nigerian music star Asake discussed his love for money and how it plays a crucial role in his lifestyle. Asake explained that money d...

item.title: Rising Voices from Bicol: The Growth of Young Artists Outside Metro Manila
item.description: This article from ABS-CBN News, written by Winkie Bernaldez, explores how young artists from the Bicol Region are beginning to establish their creative presence beyond the influence of Metro Manila. T...

item.title: Impact of Israel's Attack on South Pars Gas Field and Its Global Consequences
item.description: On March 18, 2026, Israel launched an attack on Iran's South Pars gas field, the largest in the world, which is shared with Qatar across the Persian Gulf. In retaliation, Iran struck energy facilities...

item.title: Senate Says E No Fit Comment on Dual Party Membership Bill Until House Transmit Am
item.description: The Nigerian Senate don clear air on why dem never take position on the bill wey dey aim to criminalise dual membership for political parties. According to Senator Yemi Adaramodu, wey be Chairman of S...

item.title: Impact of Iran Conflict on African Economies and Security
item.description: As the Iran war dey intensify, African governments dey watch the matter with heavy concern. Conflicts for Gulf normally no stay local, because economic effects dey spread fast through global energy ma...

item.title: Banff National Park sees record 4.5 million visitors in 2025–26 amid growing tourism challenges
item.description: Banff National Park has set yet another attendance record, welcoming 4.5 million visitors during the 2025–26 fiscal year. This marks a steady rise in visitation over the past decade and surpasses the ...

item.title: Robert Mueller, Former FBI Director and Key Figure in Russia Probe, Dies at 81
item.description: Robert Mueller, the former FBI director who led the investigation into Russian interference in the 2016 US election and its connections to the Trump campaign, has died at the age of 81. Mueller, a dec...

item.title: Northern Territory communities brace for more flooding after ex-cyclone Narelle
item.description: Ex-tropical cyclone Narelle has continued to bring destructive weather across northern Australia, posing new flood risks for communities already battered by weeks of heavy rain. The system, once a cat...

item.title: Rivers receding across B.C. south coast after atmospheric river brings heavy rain and minor flooding concerns
item.description: Residents along British Columbia’s south coast are relieved as rivers begin to recede following several days of intense rainfall from a multiday atmospheric river weather system. The B.C. River Foreca...

item.title: Senators Earn Convincing 5-2 Win Over Short-Handed Maple Leafs
item.description: The Ottawa Senators delivered a commanding performance against the Toronto Maple Leafs on Saturday night, securing a 5-2 victory over their provincial rivals. Goaltender Anthony Stolarz was slated to ...
